expectations.text: Spec file uses YYYY-MM-DD-*/SPEC.md naming convention under .specs/features/
expectations.evidence: File created at .specs/features/2026-03-10-payment-retry-logic/SPEC.md — exact naming convention.

expectations.text: .specs/ directory is initialized with features/, bugs/, and AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: .specs/AGENTS.md exists with full agent workflow guide. Directory structure .specs/features/ created.

expectations.text: Spec contains at least one [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] or [ASSUMPTION] marker
expectations.evidence: Multiple markers: [ASSUMPTION: Use 3 total attempts], [ASSUMPTION: Retry scheduling occurs on subsequent RunBillingCycle], [OPEN QUESTION: choose retry-state persistence medium], [RISK: Retry metadata added to ChargeResult may impact consumers]. At least 10 markers total.

expectations.text: Technical Approach section references real codebase files (charge.go, subscription.go, or stripe_client.go)
expectations.evidence: Entry Points lists: internal/billing/subscription.go, internal/billing/charge.go, internal/billing/stripe_client.go. Also marks NEW: internal/billing/retry_store.go and NEW: internal/billing/charge_retry_test.go.

expectations.text: Spec includes a populated Out of Scope / Non-Goals section with at least one item
expectations.evidence: 4 items in Out of Scope: smart retry strategy, dunning workflows, refactoring UpgradePlan bug, real-time async queue infrastructure.

expectations.text: Spec includes an Alternatives Considered section with at least one alternative
expectations.evidence: 3 alternatives: inline immediate retries (rejected), delegate to Stripe smart retries (deprioritized), do nothing (rejected).

expectations.text: Verification section contains the exact text 'IMPLEMENTING AGENT' in an HTML comment
expectations.evidence: Lines 86-88: '<!-- IMPLEMENTING AGENT: You MUST check each box as you complete it and run every command listed below. An unchecked box = incomplete work. -->'

expectations.text: Probing/discovery questions were generated before the spec draft
expectations.evidence: probing_questions.md contains 3 questions (2 Critical, 1 High) with proper scoreboard format: 'Remaining: Critical: 2 | High: 1 | Medium: 0'. Questions reference specific code locations.

expectations.text: Spec identifies that ChargeResult is consumed by analytics, fraud-detection, or admin dashboard
expectations.evidence: Probing question #2 explicitly states 'ChargeResult is documented as consumed by analytics, fraud-detection, and the admin dashboard'. Risk section also references this: 'Retry metadata added to ChargeResult may impact analytics/fraud/admin consumers'.
